game_info.introduction_text: You awaken and everything is blurry. You sit up, rub your eyes and notice that you somehow have arrived in the middle of a beach. Your clothes are torn, and your throat is a little sore. Slowly you get up to your feet and look around. This is the start of a long and wonderful journey. You must use logic, as well as wits and courage. You don't know how you got here, but maybe you can get home.
game_info.output_history:
You awaken and everything is blurry. You sit up, rub your eyes and notice that you somehow have arrived in the middle of a beach. Your clothes are torn, and your throat is a little sore. Slowly you get up to your feet and look around. This is the start of a long and wonderful journey. You must use logic, as well as wits and courage. You don't know how you got here, but maybe you can get home.

You are standing on a large beachfront. In front of you looms a great forest, populated with a wide assortment of trees, including a few abnormally tall ones. You notice that the western portion of this island is made up of a series of cliffs and rocky terrain that gently slopes down towards the lush green portions of the island to the east.

You can see a shady spot under a tree to the NorthWest, a forest starts to the NorthEast, and the beach extends both SouthWest and SouthEast. There is also a path that leads into the forest to the North.

133.description_short: You are standing in front of the exposed roots of a large tree.
133.name: Entrance to Underground
133.description_long: You are facing are large tree which appears to have a hollow trunk. There is a gap between the tree's root that you believe you can just barely fit through. The gap appears to lead down into the base of the tree.
133.direction_description: You can head Down in between the roots of the tree, or South to the base of one of the largest trees in the forest.

132.description_short: You are standing in a large spherical chamber.
132.name: Inner Chamber of the Temple of the White Orb
132.description_long: The circular doorway opens up into a large spherical chamber. From the lowest dip of the curved floor a cylindrical pedestool rises up, terminating at the exact center of the room, where four metal spokes rise from the top and curve inward in proportion to the rest of the rooms dimensions.
132.direction_description: The only exit to this room leads to the South.

131.description_short: You are in the entrance to a temple.
131.name: Entrance to the Temple of the White Orb
131.description_long: The interior of this temple is a smooth, square tunnel leading deep into the heart of the mountain. Shafts of light slice down from long shafts cut through the ceiling of the tunnel, illuminating ancient dust particles that rise into the air with your every footstep. The floor, ceiling, and walls of the tunnel are a dull white, dulled and greyed by the the ravages of time and thick films of dust. To the north of you a circular doorway leads into the heart of the temple.
131.direction_description: You can travel deeper into the temple by heading North through the circular doorway, or walk up the stairs and out into the light by moving South.

130.description_short: You are standing shin-deep in an underground river.
130.notes: Add DO(E) in regards to light
130.name: Between Waterhole and Decline
130.description_long: You are standing shin-deep in an underground river that flows through a tunnel from the west to the east. To the west a soft, gentle light filters down from a hole in the ceiling, whereas to the east is nothing but the pitch-blackness of an enternal subterraignian night.
130.direction_description: You would not be able to travel any further East away from the light spiling down from the hole in the ceiling to the West without anything but the un-aided naked eye.

129.description_short: You are on a ledge overhanging the river.
129.name: Ledge over Mountain River
129.description_long: You are now standing on a small ledge that juts out from the cliff on the northern side of the river. Glancing up, you can see a tree stump just barely overhanging the edge of the cliff, far above your head. You'd be willing to bet that there would be an excellent view from up there, but you couldn't possibly climb up there with just your bare hands.
129.direction_description: You can't climb Up the cliff with your bare hands, so you will have to climb back Down from the ledge, into the river.

128.description_short: You are treading water under a bridge.
128.name: Water under the bridge
128.description_long: You are now treading water underneath the bridge that crosses the lagoon.  There are many reeds and slimy plants growing between you and the eastern shore of the lagoon,  so you wouldn't be able to swim that way.
128.direction_description: You may swim North towards a small coral reef, South to the water's edge, or if you were better at holding your breathe under water, you might consider swimming down below the water's surface.

127.description_short: You are walking along the cliffs.
127.name: North-North-West Portion of Island
127.description_long: You are walking along the cliffs that make up the northern portion of the island.
127.direction_description: You may travel South to a well, or West or East along the cliff.

126.notes: The first of the new rooms, added to the original game in order to provide better balance.
126.name: Inside Alcove
126.description_long: You are standing inside a small alcove, along the southern shore of the island. This small space between the rocks blocks you from the ocean winds, and keeps out the spray of crashing waves. You sit here and take a few minutes to gather up your thoughts.
126.direction_description: You may only return to the East.

125.description_short: You are at the end of the game.
125.notes: The last of the original rooms. All of the following rooms have been added to The Cycon Odyssey in order to better balance the game.
125.name: CAVE
125.description_long: You now stand before a huge cave entrance that leads underground. You hear some sort of mechanism churning deep below the earth. You sigh, take a deep breathe, and step forward. You've come a long way, and there's no turning back now.
125.direction_description: This concludes the first chapter of The Cycon Oddyssey. You have sucessfully overcome many obstacles and mental challanges. I regret that you had to come so far, and you still don't know where you are, how you go here, or why you were brought here You don't even know what the name "Cycon" means. If you enjoyed this game (or even if you didn't) look for The Cycon Odyssey II, 'Tracing Roots', possibly coming soon to a server near you.

124.description_short: You are on a ledge inside of a volcano.
124.notes: You need to tie the metal cable onto the metal ring to reach the ledge below you. This room was completely modified later to better round out the game.
124.name: Upper Ledge of Volcano
124.description_long: You are now standing on a ledge that juts out of the interior wall of a volcano. The heat here is intense beyond explanation or comprehension, and begin to feel a little dizzy. Adding curiousity to this exotic locale, a large grey metal ring hangs from the ceiling at the end of the hand-carved volcanic cave.
124.direction_description: You have no interest in jumping Down into the lava below you, so you may only return South, to the cool ocean air at the other end of the volcanic cave.

123.description_short: You are on a ledge, at the northwestern corner of the island.
123.name: Large Steps
123.description_long: You are able to climb about halfway down the series of ledges before the intervals between ledges becomes too great for you to work your way down any further. There are are many large birds flying around lazily, some of which have roosted in spaces where small crags jut out from the cliff.
123.direction_description: You may only climb back Up to the top of the cliff.

122.description_short: You are on top of a volcano.
122.notes: In eariler versions of the game the metal rod was just randomly poking out of the ground here.
122.name: Top of Volcano
122.description_long: You now stand on the top of a volcano, peering down at the lava flowing deep within it. The heat emanating from within the bowels of the earth blast your face with a ferousity that causes you to snap your head back. Before averting you eyes, you happen to glance a certain dark splotch off to the side of the volcano's interior.
122.direction_description: You can go Down the north side of the volcano, or SouthEast down a path that wraps around to the southern face.

121.description_short: You are on the lower ledge inside of a volcano.
121.notes: The Red Orb is here. You need to have to bucket in order to pick it up.
121.name: Lower Ledge Inside Volcano
121.description_long: You have decended down the metal cable, down even closer to the already unbearable heat of the volcano. You are now standing on yet another ledge inside the volcano, on the verge of collapse from heat stroke. Your head is reeling with the warmth of the molten lava splashing below you.
121.direction_description: You may only climp back Up the way you came.

120.description_short: You are at the top of a hand-carved staircase that leads up the side of a mountain.
120.name: Exterior of the Temple of the White Orb
120.description_long:
You are standing at the top of the hand-carved staircase that leads up the side of a mountain. You have reached a wide open, flat, circular area which is covered with square stone tiles. Pilliars of various heights and widths are placed around the vicinity.
	At the center of a partiuclar square on the ground are four smaller stone tiles, shaped into quarter-circles, providing a stark geometric contrast to the square tiles. Behind four quarter-circle tiles stands another circular pillar, this one much thicker than the others that encircle this area.

	On top of each of the pillars that line the area are situated various three-dimensional geometric shapes that have been carved out of either glass or crystal, something which you can not be sure about due to their height above you. Regardless of the material, each work is an elaborate and beautiful piece, whose delicate curves catch and reflect the sun's light. Rainbows of varying hues are cast all about this area, but especially upon the center pillar.

	In the center of the large center pillar, a large sphereical container (made from a material similar to the geomtric shapes that line the other pillars) is half-filled with a clear liquid that glissens in the rays of light. Directly before you is a short and stubby cylindrical column with extremely elaborate marks and patterns etched onto its surface. Behind you to the south, the hand-carved staircase leads Down the mountain to the clifftops below.

119.description_short: You are in the middle of a rope bridge.
119.name: Middle of Bridge
119.description_long: You are now walking across a rickety rope bridge. Below you is a deep gulley with the ocean's waves crashing at the bottom.
119.direction_description: The bridge goes East and West.

118.description_short: You are in the entrance to a volcanic cave.
118.notes: Original Text went as follows: You are resting inside a naturally-formed dugout, probably shaped and molded by the lava the last time the volcano erupted. The dugout resemble a very small cave, about seven feet deep into the volcano, six feet high, and six feet wide. Its deep enough to block out the sun, but wide enough to let in the refreshing sea breeze.
118.name: Cave Entrance
118.description_long: What appeared to be a small dugout from the path below you actually turns out to be a carefully carved cave leading into the heart of the volcano. Heat from the molten lava within the depts of the volcano rolls out across your face, a distinctly different sensation from the cool ocean breeze blowing across your back. The walls of the cave are perfectly smooth, and the shape of the cave has been prefectly rounded. The cave continues to the north, where a small ledge juts out from the volcano's interior wall.
118.direction_description: You can walk North towards the heart of the fiery volcano, or climb back Down to the path that winds around it's southern face.

117.description_short: You are a the highest point of the northwester end of the island.
117.name: Top of Large Steps
117.description_long: You are now standing at the highest point of the northwestern end of the island. Below you a series of ledges drop off at intervals towards the craggy rocks below, forming a sort of natural "staircase" leading down to the crashing waves below.
117.direction_description: You can try to climb Down the series of ledges to where the cliffs meet the ocean, or head back along the top of the cliffs to the East.

116.description_short: You are at one end of a rope bridge.
116.name: Western Side of Chasm
116.description_long: In front of you lies a deep chasm, with waves crashing at the bottom. A rope bridge dangles listlessly down the side of the opposite cliff, having been detached from this side. There are two pegs in the ground here, identical to the ones the bridge is attached to on the other side.
116.direction_description: You may go West between the mountains and northern cliffs of the island, or East across the rope bridge.

115.description_short: You are standing under a small shaft of light, knee-deep in a river that flows through an underground tunnel.
115.notes: Up should lead to room 107 - Waterhole (Well) after crank is moved
115.name: Bottom of Waterhole
115.description_long: You now standing knee-deep in an underground river that flows through an underground tunnel. The air smells strongly of dank rock and saltwater. A gentle light drifts down from the end of a long circular shaft in the ceiling of the tunnel. The light is sufficient for you to see around you, but only dim enough to permit black and white night vision. Just below the surface of the water you can see many flat round objects that are made out of a metal that has rusted away to nothing but delicate wafers. If they were once coins they are most certainly useless now, as they completely flake apart whenever you try to pick one up for a closer examination.
115.direction_description: You may travel East or West through the tunnel, though you can't see very far in either direction because the light from above doesn't help to illuminate very far to either side of you. The walls of the shaft leading Up through the ceiling are too slick for you climb up with your bare hands.

114.description_short: You are standing ankle-deep in an underground river.
114.notes: There needs to be a descriptive DO(D) here between 114 and 52. Also insert anoher Obstruction in regards to the light situation.
114.name: Passway
114.description_long: You are standing ankle-deep in a river that flows from the west to the east through an underground tunnel. The ceiling and floor of the underground tunnel declines below the waterline to the east. You would have to be very confident in your ability to hold your breath if you wanted to follow the river in that direction, since you would have to be swimming underwater. You certainly wouldn't want to risk being trapped underground, in a water-filled tunnel, struggling against the current trying to find the surface! To the west of you the river is bathed in complete darkness.
114.direction_description: You are in a very precarious situation, as you would need light to be able to move to the West, and you would have to hold your breath for an unknown period of time if you wanted to swim Down under the water.

113.description_short: You are swimming along the bottom of the ocean.
113.name: Bottom of Ocean
113.description_long: You are swimming along the bottom of the ocean, observing various species of fish that you have never seen before. Many exotic forms of		 coral grow here as well.
113.direction_description: You can only swim back towards the surface.

112.notes: Dock level(100) -> Water Suface(68) -> Underwater/Shark(112) -> Ocean floor(113)
112.name: Underwater/Shark
112.description_long: You are swimming underwater. The sun's rays of light bounce off the water's surface, shimmering above you.
112.direction_description: You can either swim Up to the water's surface, or Down to the ocean floor.

111.name: Behind Volcano
111.description_long: You are now standing on the northern side of a large volcano that takes up the majority of the southwestern portion of the island.
111.direction_description: You can either go Up to the top of the volcano, or West towards a set of winding stairs.

110.description_short: You are in an underground river.
110.notes:
Might want to change direction river flows. Too many turns.
Can't go North unless you have light.
110.name: Light to the East
110.description_long: The tunnel banks gently here, with the water flowing from the north and moving eastward, where a gentle light filters down from a hole in the ceiling. The waterlevel is deeper to the north, changing from hip level to knee deep as the water flows. The water flows out of complete darkness, to the point where moving south would be an impossible task with nothing but the naked eye.
110.direction_description: You can go East towards the light, or deeper into the tunnel to the North.

109.description_short: You are on the western bank of a river.
109.name: Opposite Bank
109.description_long: You are standing on the western bank of a river, behind a large cliff. This bank of the river inclines sharply into the distance to the west.
109.direction_description: You can either go West up the large hill, or East across the river.

108.description_short: You are facing a large wall.
108.name: The Wall
108.description_long: You are now facing the southern side of a long wall that divides the western half of the island. This particular section of the wall has begun to fall into disrepare, after what appears to be many years of neglect. Some of the bricks have become somewhat loose, and you feel a little wary of anything that might fall from above, causing you to glance up towards the top of the wall repeatedly. To the west of you the wall twists to the south and forms a staircase that leads into the northern face of a small volcano. To the east the ground drops off sharply, and nothing but weeds and thorned bushes cover the land.
108.direction_description: Between the wall in front of you and the poor terraign to the east, you may only return South, to the bottom of the the twisting staircase.

107.description_short: You are in front of a well.
107.notes:
The rope and bucket will later be detatchable, the crank will not. Bucket Starts UP!
Down will lead to Room 115 - Bottom of Waterhole after crank is used
107.name: Waterhole (Well)
107.description_long: There is a small uncovered well here, in the middle of the green pasture. You can hear water flowing at the bottom, and the smell of saltwater hangs in the air. Wide cliffs overhang the ocean below to the north, while to the south a path gently twists it's way through a pasture and up a hill. A small wooden structure is built into the well, with a crank system for raising an lowering a bucket into the water below.
107.direction_description: From the well, you can travel North towards the northern cliffs of the island, or south along the faint path that works its way uphill across the pasture.

106.description_short: You are on a twisting staircase.
106.name: Twisting Stairs
106.description_long: You are standing at the bottom of a staircase that twists to the east as it rises from the northern face of a small volcano. To the north, the staircase has merged into a long wall that run east across this half of the island. To the east lies the northern face of a small volcano that forms the majority of the south western island.
106.direction_description: You can either go Up the staircase, North towards the wall that stretches across the western portion of the island, or East towards the northern face of the volcano.

105.description_short: You are standing in front of a tunnel that has been blocked off by a stone wall.
105.notes: This room with have to been remodified after the destruction of the stone wall.
105.name: Stone Wall/Steps
105.description_long: There is the beginning of a tunnel that leads upwards from this large underground room, but you cannot enter it as it has been blocked off by a stone wall.
105.direction_description: The stone wall blocking the tunnel leading Upwards prevents you from being able to move in any other direction but South.

104.description_short: You are on top of a cliff
104.name: Top of Cliff
104.description_long: After all of that climbing to get up here, you realize that this would be a perfect place to take a short rest. Apparently, someone else had the same idea, for there is a small campsite off to the east. In the meantime, you sit back and relax, taking in the view.
104.direction_description: You can either go East to a campsite, or Down to the ledge below.

103.description_short: You are at the southwestern-most point of the island.
103.name: Craggy Rocks Below
103.description_long: You are standing at the southwestern point of the island, taking in the magnificent view. Below you the waves crash against the craggy rocks. Your gaze out over the deep blue water until you decide that you've had enough sight-seeing and that it is time to move on.
103.direction_description: You can only return to the East.

102.description_short: You are in a river that runs through an underground tunnel.
102.notes: The Black Orb used to be here
102.name: Middle of Tunnel
102.description_long: You are wading hip-deep through a cold underground river, feeling your way along the walls of the tunnel, which are slick with strange subterraignean plantlife that somehow manage to thrive without light between the rock and saltwater. The river bends here, with the water flowing from in from the east and continuing on to the south.
102.direction_description: The tunnel turns both South and East here.

101.description_short: You are standing in a river, at the entrance to a tunnel.
101.name: Leads Into Tunnel
101.description_long: The river enters a tunnel here. You can hear the echo of the water trickling within. Inside of the tunner it is pitch black, and you can't see any light at the end.
101.direction_description: It's far too dark for you to venture East into the tunnel with your unaided eyes, so you will have to turn against the current to the West.

100.description_short: You are standing on a wooden dock.
100.name: Dock
100.description_long: You are standing on a wooden dock that stretches out over the ocean. You glance out across the sea as the waves crash all around you. You can't help but to feel a little homesick as the sun's rays dazzle your eyes with gleaming reflections.
100.direction_description: You may dive Down into the water, or head West to where the dock extends from the land.

99.description_short: You are at the mouth of a river.
99.name: Mouth of River
99.description_long: The river takes in its water from the sea here. The current is quite a bit stronger than before, and it pushes you backwards.
99.direction_description: Since the current is so strong, you may only go South.

98.description_short: You are in the river that runs through the mountains.
98.name: Runs Through Mountains
98.description_long: As you glance upwards at the cliffs on the north side of the river, you notice that they're somewhat lower than the ones on the south side. A ledge juts out from the cliff above you to the north, providing a place for you to rest and dry off, if you so desired.
98.direction_description: You can climb Up to the ledge above you, or you can follow the river to the East where it bends northward, or to the West where it goes underground.

97.description_short: You are at a point where the river turns.
97.name: Turning of River
97.description_long: At this point, the river turns sharply to the west. You can also see a tunnel further down to the west, where the river must go underground.
97.direction_description: You can follow the river North or West.

96.description_short: You are wading along a river.
96.name: Undergrowth
96.description_long: As you wade along the river, you notice that the undergrowth is growing thicker here. To the south, the river takes a bend to the west. The cliffs above you are begining to incline the farther along the river you go.
96.direction_description: You can go South to where the river turns, or North where a set of rocks crosses it.

95.description_short: You are at the northern end of an immense underground room.
95.notes: This room TL may have to be dynamically modified after the stone wall is broken, as will room 67.
95.name: Altar of the Black Orb
95.description_long: This end of the large underground room is mainly taken up by a large stone statue of a human skeleton that is bound to an ornate alter of some sort. The statue is placed upright against the wall of the room, giving the impression that you were floating in space above the remains of the victim's body. Apparently this room was once used for some form of human sacrifice. At the northeastern end of this room lies a smaller tunnel which appears to have been closed off by the building of a stone wall.
95.direction_description: You can head either South across this large underground room, or NorthEast towards the stone-off tunnel passage.

94.description_short: You are in an underground tunnel.
94.name: Tunnel (Need Flashlight)
94.description_long: You are in an underground tunnel, formed by a river that eroded its way through the cliffs that make up most of the western portion of the island. The current is pulling you away from the entrance.
94.direction_description: You may travel deeper into the tunnel by moving West, or out into the light by going East.

93.description_short: You are on a staircase that leads into the top of a wall.
93.name: Stairs Leading Into Top of Wall
93.description_long: You are standing on a set of stairs that rotate to the east, where they merge into the top of a long wall. At the bottom, the staircase merges into the northern face of a small volcano.
93.direction_description: You can either go Down to the bottom of the stairs, or Up to where they lead into the top of a wall.

92.description_short: You are standing in front of the remains of a once great manor.
92.name: Ruins of the Manor
92.description_long: You are standing on a cobblestone path that leads in between a tall greyish-white wall and the immense ruins of a once-great manor. The odor of decaying wood drifts radiates through the air from the remains of the old building to the east. You have absolutely no desire to crawl around that dirty heap of debris. The cobblestone path continues south, towards the partial remains of a much smaller building, nestled in between the wall and a few bushes, overgrown with weeds.
92.direction_description: You can exit through the gate to the west, or follow the path to the south, towards the remains of the smaller building.

91.description_short: You are south of a well.
91.name: South of Hole (Well)
91.description_long: You are standing in the middle of a soft path that lightly weaves its way across a wide-open green pasture. To the north of you stands a small well, and the path works its way towards the bottom of a gentle hill to the southwest.
91.direction_description: You can go North to the well, or SouthWest along the path to the bottom of a hill.

90.description_short: You are standing on a rock formation in the middle of a river.
90.name: Rock Steps

2.transition_text: You wade your way against the rivers current to a small patch of soggy ground just north of where you started.

90.description_long: You are standing in the middle of a small formation of rocks that lead across the river. This river, after years of constant pressure, has eroded a small passway through the huge cliffs that make up most of the northern and western portions of the island. The river takes water in from the ocean and follows a winding route through the rock to the south.
90.direction_description: You can go North or South along the river, or carefully step across the rocks to reach the Eastern or Western banks of the river.

89.description_short: You are at the bottom of a hill.
89.name: Bottom of Hill
89.description_long: You are standing at the bottom of a hill in a large pasture. A path leads to a well northeast of here.
89.direction_description: You can go NorthEast along the path that leads to a well, or walk up the hill to the South.

88.description_short: You are on a hill, in a pasture.
88.name: Down Hill
88.description_long: You are walking on a hill that gently slopes its way down through a green pasture. At the bottom of the hill a barely-perceptable path gently eases its way northward.
88.direction_description: You can either go North to the bottom of the hill, or walk South to the top of it.

87.name: Pasture
87.description_long: You are on the top of a large hill that gently slopes its way down through a pasture to the north of you. To the south and east of you a greyish-white wall wraps its way around you. To the west the hill ends and a cliff drops down to the crashing ocean below.
87.direction_description: You can go walk North down the hill, NorthEast to a gate built into the wall east of here, or climb Up onto the top of the wall behind you.

86.description_short: You are at the western end of a large wall.
86.name: Western End of Wall
86.description_long: As the wall approaches the the cliffs at the edge of the western portion of the island, the top winds around to the south and sinks into the ground in the form of a staircase. The bottom of this "staircase" leads into the northern face of the volcano that makes up the south western portion of the island.
86.direction_description: You can travel Down the staircase, or East along the top of the wall.

85.description_short: You are in front of a large metal gate.
85.name: Gate
85.description_long: You are standing on the northeastern side of a low hill that gently slopes its way down to a green pasture. There are two long walls running east-west and north-south across the top of the hill. You are standing in front of a large metal gate that has been built into the wall that runs north-south across the hill. There is an enormous and elaborately-decorated lock with a large keyhole centered in the middle of the gate. You can see a cobblestone path leading towards the immense ruins of a once great manor on the other side of the gate.
85.direction_description: You can go SouthWest along the top of the hill, or East through the gate, if it is open.

84.notes: Not used in the final game.
84.name: Death
84.description_long: Oops! You know you must have done *something* wrong once the rigor mortis begins to set in.
84.direction_description: For some reason you just feel like lying here for awhile.

83.description_short: You are at the start of a rope bridge.
83.name: Start of Rope Bridge
83.description_long: Here at the top of the hill, a broken rope bridge dangles down into the chasm between two cliffs. The bridge is attached to two pegs on this side, and you can see the pegs on the other side, where the bridge used to be attached.
83.direction_description: You can travel West to cross the rope bridge, or East down the slope of a riverbank.

82.description_short: You are at the start of a wooden dock.
82.name: Start of Dock
82.description_long: The peninsula ends where a wooden dock stretches out over the water.
82.direction_description: You can either go East onto the dock, Down into the water, or West to where the peninsula turns south.

81.name: Soft Sand
81.description_long: The peninsula bends to the east here, leading out towards a wooden dock extends out over the water.
81.direction_description: You may head East to the start of a wooden dock, or South, to the start of the peninsula.

80.description_short: You are standing on a peninsula.
80.name: Penninsula
80.description_long: You're standing at the southern tip of a peninsula that extends from the northeastern end of the island. The peninsula bends to the east just north of here, where it continues for a short distance before terminating in a wooden dock.
80.direction_description: You can go to the North where the peninsula bends eastward, or South to the northern face of a sand dune.

79.description_short: You are standing admist the ruins of an old toolshed.
79.name: Stubble - Sledge
79.description_long: You are standing admist the ruins of an old, small building. It would appear that it was once an old toolshed, but simply crumbled from lack of upkeep. Many tools are stewn about the wreckage, of various shapes, sizes, and usages with which you are entirely unfamiliar. Some of the tools have decayed and rusted to point of utter uselesness, some other are in only slightly better condition.
79.direction_description: You may only go back North along the cobblestone road.

78.description_short: You are on top of a high wall.
78.name: High Wall
78.description_long: You edge your way along the portion of the wall that splits off to the north. The ground below you drops off rather rapidly. As you face north, there is a pasture far below to the left, and to the right lies the remains of a small building.
78.direction_description: Since the wall is much too high on either side for you to jump down, and to the north the top of the wall ends where a gate begins, you can only go back to the South.

77.description_short: You are in the middle of a river.
77.name: River
77.description_long: You are standing on a small strip of soggy land in the middle of a river that draws it's water from the ocean. The river has eroded its way south through the rockface.
77.direction_description: You can wade North towards the mouth of the river, or South towards a small rock formation that crosses the river.

76.description_short: You are in the middle of a huge cavern.
76.notes: Original Text: You have uncovered a tunnel that leads downward, to a large cavern. You slowly crawl through it, and upon reaching the end, your eyes widen and you feel you jaw almost hit the floor. Much to your amazement, there is a huge flat wooden platform ahead of you. On top of the platform is a wide assortment of gears and switches and levers. Behind and above the panels is a huge plate of glass. Astonished and amazed at finding something resembling a computer on an island that seemed so primal you step forward and pull the red handle in front of you...
76.name: Organic/Analog Comuter Room
76.description_long:
You have removed the cave-in left behind in the tunnel to the south of you, and followed it into an enormous underground cavern, something that presents a spectacle unlike anything that you've ever seen before. 

	The tunnel leads out onto a ledge that reaches halfway out into the cavern. There are various holes far above your head in the stone ceiling of the cavern, which filter in shafts of light that fall at obscure angles onto various locations throughout the entire area.

	The far wall of the cavern reaches down from the top of the ceiling to far beyond your range of vision, down into the depths below. There is a steady stream of water cascading down the far wall, and in certain areas small mechanisms which resemble the balances of a scale capture some of the streams running down the rockface into small cups, which cause the opposite sides of the balance to rise and pour water into other devices. The reaction is that when water runs down a certain area of the wall, a chain reaction between the devices is enacted in one direction, and then as the water spills out from the cups, a new chain reaction develops across another portion of the large chamber.

	The air is fill with the sound of gushing water, and the rapid clicking of many of these devices, as their cups fill and the balances switch to the other side. In a few places, some sort of crystal is placed upon the top of the balance. If the water falls in a particular pattern, the crystal will cross into the path of a beam of light that falls from the ceiling above, causing new rays of light to refract in other directions, sometimes touching other scales, and sometimes refracting off stationary crystals imbeded directly into the various walls of the cavern. Such activities continue all the way down the far cavern wall, for as far as your eye can  see.

	Across the abyss below you lies another platform to the west. On this ledge a series of gears and levers are imbeded into the rock, perhaps to control the flow of the water above, and therefore the visual patterns created by the balances and beams of light.

	The overall effect of this grandeous machincal process quickly begins to overwhelm you senses with a sense of awe and appreciation for the machine's beauty.

	At the end of the platform on which you are standing are four different pedestools, each with a consistent height approximately equal to the level of your shoulders. There is a beam of light falling from the ceiling above that crosses just above the top of each of these pedestools, at varying angles. Each of the pedestools is of a slightly differnt hue, one white, one black, one blue, and one red.
76.direction_description: You may only return North, to the area where the cave-in took place.

75.description_short: You are at the southern end of an immense underground room.
75.notes: It is possible that the second game could be connected to the first by means of this pit. The foul reek and stench could be from the remains of previous sacrifices, something which the player discovers at some point during a later quest.
75.name: Giant Worm
75.description_long: The tunnel spreads out into an immense room here. There's a slab of flat stone in the middle, with a deep pit behind it. There is a large pile of dirt caked around the corner of an enormous tunnel at the northwestern corner of the room. A foul stench seems to be eminating from the pit behind the stone slab.
75.direction_description: You can head North across the large room, or South into a series of underground tunnels.

74.description_short: You are in an underground tunnel that widens to the north.
74.name: Sounds to the North
74.description_long: The tunnel continues to the north here, to the point where the fungi growing in the soil are insufficient to provide enough light for you to make your way through the tunnel system with nothing but the naked eye. Unless you can provide an additional source of light, it is too dark for you to continue following the tunnel to the north.
74.direction_description: The tunnel leads North into the darkness, and South to where it forks and meets up with another tunnel system.

73.description_short: You are standing in and underground tunnel, in front of a cave-in.
73.name: Cave-In
73.description_long: This tunnel, which continues to the south has been blocked by some sort of cave-in not long ago. A few large boulders and pieces of debris have fallen from above, in such a fashion that could not be removed by hand.
73.direction_description: You can follow the tunnel to the North where it forks, or to the South, should you be able to remove the rubble left behind by the cave-in.

72.description_short: You are standing in an underground tunnel, which is inlade with a stone surface on one wall.
72.name: Wall Painting
72.description_long: This branch of the underground tunnel system is somewhat wider than the tunnel the southwest, allowing you just enough room to stand up fully erect. The walls of the this tunnel are far more solid as well. On the eastern wall of this particular section a large, flat, stone surface has been inlade. On the surface of the stone are various markings and pictures of human-shaped creatures involved in what appears to be a ritual of some sort, but that is about all that you can make out for certain. The tunnel continues to the north and to the south, and connects with a smaller tunnel system to the southwest.
72.direction_description: You can either follow the Northern, Southern, or SouthWestern tunnels.

71.description_short: You are in a small subterrainian chamber at the end of an underground passageway.
71.notes: Original Text : The walls of this room themselves cast an eerie glow, perhaps from either a bizarre element imbedded in the rock, or some sort of phosphorous fungus. That eerie glow is what has been providing you with light. There is a large stone placed in the middle of the room.
71.name: Glowing Spear
71.description_long: The underground tunnel leading downwards widens out here into a small subterrainian chamber. The small species of fungus that grows in the soil of these tunnels seems to grow in a slightly larger form all around the walls of this chamber, creating a much higher level of light than is present in the narrow passageways. In the center of this room sits a small stone pedestool, which is covered with various forms of bizarre pictures and markings, whose possible meaning utterly eludes you.
71.direction_description: The only direction that you can go is back South through the tunnel that led you here.

70.description_short: You are in an underground tunnel that leads downward.
70.name: Lowering Passage
70.description_long: You are crawling along an underground passageway that declines to the north. The light cast by the fungi growing in the soil appears to grow stronger the further down into the earth you continue. Ahead to the north you just make out that this particular tunnel appears to terminate as it enters a small subterraignian chamber. To the south, the tunnel rises to a point where it forks into three directions.
70.direction_description: You can go North to the room at the end of the tunnel, or South to where the tunnel forks.

69.description_short: You are at a point where the underground tunnel forks.
69.notes: Original Text : As you continue to crawl through the tunnel, it occurs to you that although you can still see, you should have run out of light a long time ago. You simply dismiss it, and continue. After a few feet you notice that the tunnel's ceiling is getting almost high enough for you to stand up straight and walk. You stop moving when you see reach a point where the tunnel forks.
69.name: Tunnel Forks
69.description_long: As you continue to crawl through the tunnel, you pause for a moment to take a closer look at the walls of this passageway. The entire tunnel system is dimly lit, though there is no single source of origin for the light. Examinging the soil in detail, you can see what appears to be millions of tiny phosphorus fungi that appear to glow with a yellowish inner light. This is very fortunate for you, as you would otherwise be totally unable to find your way around by using your naked eyes alone. Taking another moment to better orient yourself, you see that the underground tunnel system forks here in three directions.
69.direction_description: One passage continues North and heads at a angle slightly sloping downward angle, another goes NorthEast, and the last passage continues to the South.

68.name: Shark
68.description_long: You are treading water next to a short wooden dock.
68.direction_description: You climb Up onto the dock, or you might consider swimming Down into the ocean depths, if only you had a little more confidents in your ability to hold your breathe underwater.

67.description_short: You are behind the sand dune.
67.notes: After you use the sledgehammer to destroy the cement wall at the northern end of the underground tunnel, sand will pour down into the tunnel, revealing a staircase that leads up to this side of the sand dune. Down(105)
67.name: Behind Dune (Doors)
67.description_long: You are standing on the north side of a small sand dune. Beyond the dune, a small peninsula extends from the island, out over the deep blue sea.
67.direction_description: You can go North to the peninsula, or South to the other side of the dune.

66.description_short: You are on the end of the beach.
66.name: Edge of Beach
66.description_long: The sand on this beach is very gritty, with many weeds growing in patches that thicken to the south. The ocean washes up gently along the shoreline; its waves having already been broken by a small peninsula that extends from the island east of here. The peninsula terminates into a wooden dock just north of you, across the water.
66.direction_description: You can go South to the north shore of the lagoon, or SouthEast into a dense patch of reeds.

65.description_short: You are standing in a thick patch of reeds.
65.name: Reeds
65.description_long: You are standing in the middle of a thick patch of reeds and other knee-high plants. The soil is very wet and marshy in this area, a trend that continues off to the east, where the earth becomes very swamplike. You have absolutely no desire to trudge through a swamp in you current condition, so heading east would be out of the question.
65.direction_description: You can go West to the north shore of the lagoon, or NorthWest across the reeds, towards the ocean.

64.description_short: You are on the north shore of the lagoon.
64.name: North Shore
64.description_long: The majority of the northern beach of the lagoon is taken up by a small coral reef that stretches across this side of the lagoon. To the east grows a thick patch of reeds that becomes less and less dense as it wraps around to the north. Beyond the reeds to the north, the beach extends towards the ocean.
64.direction_description: You can go North towards the ocean, West to a small sand dune, or East to where a patch of reeds are growing in the sand.

63.description_short: You are standing on the south side of a small sand dune.
63.name: Small Dune
63.description_long: The sand in this area has piled up to create a small dune. You are standing on the southern side of the dune gazing out over the lagoon to the southwest.
63.direction_description: You can go North to the other side of the dune, East along the north shore of the lagoon, or South along it's west shore.

62.description_short: You are treading water in front of a coral reef.
62.name: Coral Reef
62.description_long: A small coral reef has formed at the northern end of the lagoon, blocking your ability to swim in that direction. It is covered by a colorful assortment of coral and shellfish. The lagoon was probably a part of the ocean at one time, which would explain the saltwater.
62.direction_description: You can only swim to the South because the coral reef blocks further passage to the North.

61.description_short: You are on the northwest shore of the lagoon.
61.name: West Shore
61.description_long: The cliffs part to the west here, allowing you access to a small river that has eroded its way through the stone over the ages. The river feeds from the ocean far ahead to the north, and weaves its way through the mountainside to the south, where it takes a turn westward. To the north the wind has gathered sand from the lagoon's shore into a small sand dune, and to the east of you an exotic coral reef stretches across the lagoon.
61.direction_description: You can head West to where a group of rocks cross the river, North to the small sand dune, or South to where a long wooden bridge crosses the lagoon.

60.description_short: You are on the east shore of the lagoon.
60.name: East Shore
60.description_long: You are on the northeast shore of the lagoon. To the north and east the wet patches of sand and swampland wrap around and block your path on those sides. A beautiful coral reef is situated to the west of you, preventing you from continuing in that direction.
60.direction_description: You may only go South to the east end of the long wooden bridge.

59.description_short: You are standing next to the long wooden bridge.
59.name: Troll Gatekeeper
59.description_long: You are standing next to a long wooden bridge that spans the width of the lagoon. It appears to be the only way across the lagoon. There is a gate at the entrance  to the bridge, with a gnarled face of something that looks like a troll in it's center.
59.direction_description: You can go North along the shore of the lagoon, cross the bridge by going East, or walk alongside fo the cliff to the South.

58.description_short: You are west of the lagoon.
58.name: Sand
58.description_long: Walking alongside the cliffs, you shuffle your feet in sandy terraign. The lagoon shimmers in the sun's rays to the east of you, dazzling your peripheral vision.
58.direction_description: You can go South along the cliffs, or North towards a bridge that crosses the middle of the lagoon.

57.description_short: You are at the southwest bank of the lagoon.
57.name: Soutwest Bank
57.description_long: You are at the southwest bank of a lagoon. To the west of you, stretching northwards to the horizon runs a series of cliffs that divide the eastern and western portions of the island.
57.direction_description: You can go Northalong the cliffs, South to a sandbank, or East to the edge of the water.

56.description_short: You are in the middle of a long rope bridge.
56.name: Bridge
56.description_long: You are in the middle of a long wooden bridge that crosses the lagoon. You stare down at the water, trying to see the bottom, but its too murky, and there is too much reflection from the sun for you to be able to see much of anything.
56.direction_description: The bridge extends both East and West across the lagoon. You could also dive Down into the waters of the lagoon below you.

55.description_short: You are on the east side of the bridge that crosses the lagoon.
55.name: Opposite Bank
55.description_long: This side of the lagoon is far less attractive that the opposite bank. The sand is very wet here, and degenerates into swampland to the east. You are afraid to wander very far from the side of the lagoon, due to the possibilty getting stuck in quicksand.
55.direction_description: You are afraid of quicksand in the swampland east of here, so you may only go West across the bridge, or North or South along the east shore of the lagoon.

54.description_short: You are at the bottom of a pit.
54.notes: You can't get here until you've past the Troll
54.name: Ladder and Key
54.description_long: You are standing at the bottom of a large pit that was probably once used to capture unsuspecting creatures offguard. The dark grey bones of several such victims are strewn about in varying degrees of disarry.
54.direction_description: You may only climb back Up the ladder.

53.description_short: You are in front of a pitfall.
53.name: Ground Caves In

11.transition_text: You carefully make your way down the makeshift ladder, into the pit below.

53.description_long: You are walking around the southeastern portion of the lagoon when you come across a large hole where the ground has caved in. It looks as though this was once a trap made to catch unsuspecting creatures. A makeshift ladder leads into the depths of the pit.
53.direction_description: You can climb Down the ladder, and into the pit, or return North to where the long wooden bridge crosses the lagoon.

52.notes: The boomerang sword used to be here, as did the octopus. Also, insert a DO(D) here detailing information about the cave.
52.name: Octopus / Boomerang Sword
52.description_long: It feels as though this area has a magical aura all around it. The sun's rays of light shining down into the water seem to cause everything to sparkle. It's a shame that the saltwater hurts your eyes so much.
52.direction_description: You can barely make out what appears to be an underwater cave, far below you.You can swim Down and into it, or up to the edge of the water.

51.description_short: You are at the end of the eastern passageway.
51.name: Unclimbable Hill
51.description_long: The eastern passageway that has been etched into the mountain has apparantly not been finished in this direction, so you will have to head back the way you came.
51.direction_description: You can only go back to the West.

50.description_short: You are on the back of a winged unicorn.
50.name: Wings of Pegasus
50.description_long: After you place your lips around the small end of the conch, you close your eyes and blow. You think that you can hear something and your eyelids snap open. Before you stands a huge winged unicorn. It doesn't look frightened, so you walk over, pet its long mane, and climb up onto its back. You are now riding on the back of a beautiful winged unicorn. It reminds you of the mythological creature 'Pegasus'. You can see most of the island from up here. Apparently the western part of the island drops off about 100 feet or so, and most of the plant life is on the lower and eastern half. You can see a lagoon on the northeastern part of the island, and a volcano on the southwestern portion. The unicorn is descending now. (Please return your seats to an upright position)

49.description_short: You are on a ledge overhanging a mountain alcove.
49.name: Ledge Over Cave
49.description_long: The western passageway up the volcano ends abruptly here, after turning south and overhanging a large alcove that has been cut into the rock below you. You can neither see nor hear anything that may be down there, and if you were to jump down there you probably wouldn't break any bones, but you most certainly would not be able to climb back up to this passageway.
49.direction_description: You can either return East to where the three passways diverge, or you can chance jumping Down and finding out (for better or for worse) what lies within the aclove below you.

48.description_short: The passageways up the mountin split here.
48.name: Path Leading Upwards
48.description_long: The passageways up the mountain split off in three directions here: to the east, to the west, and to the south.
48.direction_description: You can either follow the Eastern, the Western, or the Southern passage way, which leads down to the foot of the mountain.

47.name: Roots and Tunnel
47.description_long: You are crawling through a tunnel system that leads underground through a hollow tree. The roots of the tree surround you, with a few small sprouts poking through the ceiling and dangling in front of your face. Brushing tbhis curtain of vegetative matter aside, you can just barely see that the tunnel actually forks ahead to the north.
47.direction_description: You can crawl to the North where the tunnel forks, or South towards the hollowed trunk of a large tree.

46.description_short: You are in front of a passageway through the cliffs.
46.notes: You must collect the four orbs to power the underground analog computer in order to remove the boulder.
46.name: Boulder

2.transition_text: You step around the space between the boulder and the wall of the passageway that has been cut into the cliff. You continue walking up the path to the north until the passageway terminates, and two other passages lead off to the west and to the east.

46.description_long: You are now standing before the southern face of a the island's largest mountain. What appears to be a passageway leading up the mountain has been cut sheerly into the rockface. As you run your hand along one side of the passage way, you marvel at how very smooth the surface plane feels to your touch.
46.direction_description: You can walk East along the cliff, South to the northern side of the largest tree in the forest, or North into the passageway leading up into the mountain.

45.description_short: You are next to a large cliff.
45.name: Route between boulder and sandbank
45.description_long: You are standing next to a large cliff, which blocks all passages to the north.
45.direction_description: You can go either East to a sandbank at the edge of a lagoon, or West along the rockface.

44.description_short: You are at the edge of the water.
44.name: Edge of Water
44.description_long: You are standing at the water's edge. The lagoon smells strongly of salt-water, meaning that you will have to search elsewhere for a place to find something to drink.
44.direction_description: You could go West to the southwest corner of the lagoon, South to where the  sand meets a large open pasture, or you could swim North across the lagoon, under the bridge towards a coral reef on the other side.

43.description_short: You are on top of a weak part of a wall.
43.name: Breaking Area
43.description_long: This particular portion of the wall seems rather weak to you, so you take special care and make sure of your footing as you balance on top of the wall. The ground below you to the south has sloped rapidly into a rocky terraign leading away from the volcano, and down to the southern beaches where your journey began. On the north side of the wall, a large green pasture has risen up to form a hill that meets with the wall.
43.direction_description: You can go East into the shade of tree branch, West to the western edge of the wall, or jump Down towards the pasture below you to the north.

42.name: Campsite
42.description_long: You have come across an area that looks as though someone has recently camped here.
42.direction_description: You may only go back East, to the edge of the cliff.

41.description_short: You are on top of a wall, in the shade of a large tree.
41.name: On Top of Wall
41.description_long: You are standing on the eastern end of a long wall that divides the western portion of the island, in the shade of a long branch that extends over the wall. From here you can see that the southwestern portion of the island is very rocky, the most prominant feature of which appears to be a small volcano. The northwestern portion of the island is far more flat and green, with a large grassy pasture stretching out to the horizon. Below you to the south is nothing but gnarled weeds and thorned foliage. To the north another wall splits off of the one you are standing on, forming a T shape.
41.direction_description: You can walk North to where another wall splits off, West along the current wall, or climb Up onto a branch that hangs over this end of the wall.

40.description_short: You are walking along the southern cliffs.
40.notes: The original game allowed the conch shell to take you to room 50 when blown here.
40.name: Ocean View
40.description_long: Your current position allows you to gaze out across the ocean, and relish the magnificent view.
40.direction_description: You can go West to the southwestern tip of the island, or NorthWest towards a steep drop-off point.

39.description_short: You are on a path that climbs around a volcano.
39.name: Pathway Around Volcano
39.description_long: You are walking along a pathway that corkscrews as it climbs around the small volcano that makes up the southwestern portion of the island.
39.direction_description: You may follow the path up and around to the NorthWest, or down to the SouthWest.

38.description_short: You are south of a looming volcano.
38.name: Drop-off Point
38.description_long: You are now facing north, towards a small volcano, watching as heat overflowing from the top bends and waves the the air into strange patterns. Behind you roars the ocean, as the rockface begins to drop off towards the crashing sea below.
38.direction_description: You can head North to where a pathway begins to wrap its way around to the top of the volcano, or follow the cliff to the NorthEast or SouthWest.

37.description_short: You are on a pathway up the volcano.
37.name: Pathway up Volcano
37.description_long: You are on a pathway that leads up and around a small volcano. Just a little ways up the volcano, above from the path, you can see what appears to be a small dugout emerging from the southern face of the volcano.
37.direction_description: You can climb Up to the small dugout, around the path to the NorthEast, down to the South where the pathway ends, or East away from the path to where the terraign becomes more rocky as it begins to slope down towards the west.

36.description_short: You are standing on rocky terraign.
36.name: Rocky Terraign
36.description_long: You are now standing upon the rocky terraign between the volcano and the cliffs that overhang the sandy beaches below. There are a few scragly bushes and other plants that have managed to grow here between the loose pebbles and stones, including a few thin trees, which mostly consist of dead wood.
36.direction_description: You may go South to the edge of the cliff, or West towards a pathway that climbs up and around the face of the volcano. You may also work your way Down the loose rocky terraign towards a rockslide that had occured some time ago, but it is unlikely that you would be able to climb back up.

35.description_short: You are standing admist many seashells.
35.name: Eastern End of Beach
35.description_long: You have reached the eastern end of the beach. The broken remains of various seashells lie scattered amoung the rocks.
35.direction_description: You can only go West along the beach.

34.description_short: You are inside a large hollow tree.
34.name: Inside Knothole
34.description_long: You are at the bottom of the large hollow tree; the entrance to a series of underground tunnels. Light pours in from between the gap in the tree's exposed roots above your head.
34.direction_description: You may follow the tunnel to the North, or climb Up the base of the tree and out through its exposed roots..

33.description_short: You are at the entrance to the tree.
33.notes: insert a transitional text between climbing up and down from the tree, and through the knothole as well.
33.name: Entrance to Tree
33.description_long: The tree has a large knothole in it here, probably even large enough for you to crawl into. The hole leads downward through the trunk, which you now realize has been hollowed out. A branch extends from the tree just below you, not too far from the ground.
33.direction_description: You can go South and into the hollow trunk, or out and Down the branch to the ground below.

32.description_short: You are at on a ledge.
32.name: Ledge
32.description_long: You have come to the legde extending out over the edge of the cliff, and are enjoying a spectacular view of the ocean crashing upon the beaches below.
32.direction_description: You may either walk North or SouthWest along the cliff.

31.description_short: You are at the water's edge.
31.name: Water's Edge
31.description_long: You have come to the water's edge. The soft wind, bringing in all of the smells of the ocean, feels cool across your face.

30.name: Sand Bank
30.description_long: You are standing on a sandbank which is home to many exotic species of flora.
30.direction_description: You can head North to the southwestern bank of the lagoon, East to the it's southern bank, South towards a field, or West towards one of this island's many cliffs.

29.description_short: You are south of a lagoon.
29.name: South of Lagoon
29.description_long: You are at the southern shore of a lagoon that glimmers in the sunlight. Far ahead lies a rope bridge that crosses it.
29.direction_description: You can go North to the edge of the water, West towards a sandbank, or SouthWest towards a field.

28.description_short: You are on a branch that overhangs a stone wall.
28.name: Overhanging Stone Wall
28.description_long: You are now perched upon a long, low-hanging limb that extends over the eastern end of a thick stone wall. You can't see what is below the wall from here, but the top should be wide enough for you to stand upon.
28.direction_description: You can leap Down onto the top of the stone wall below you, climb over to a branch above you to the North, or climb East to a branch below you.

27.description_short: You are standing atop a swaying branch.
27.name: Swaying Branch
27.description_long: You are hugging the trunk of a large tree, carefully maintaining your balance atop a thick branch that is swaying violently in the breeze. This constant motion so high above the ground is making you feel quite queezy, so you would like to continue moving as soon as possible. To the north of you a small wooden platform has been erected in the cleft where two branches grow out from the tree, and to the south a rather long and low-hanging limb extends out over the top of a stone wall.
27.direction_description: You can work your way North over to the platfrom or South to the branch extends over the top of a stone wall.

26.description_short: You are on a far limb.
26.name: Far Limb
26.description_long: You climb over onto one of the farther limbs of the tree. There is a branch that overhangs a stone wall to the west of you, and another down below you, although you're not certain that you'd be able to make it back up to this branch once you climbed down.
26.direction_description: You can go back to the West, or climb Down to a lower branch.

25.description_short: You are within sight of a lagoon.
25.name: Sight of Lagoon
25.description_long: The field begins to break down into sand, and you can see a lagoon off in the distance.
25.direction_description: There is a sandbank to the North, the southern shore of a lagoon to the NorthEast, and a field that spreads out to the South.

24.description_short: You are at the end of the path.
24.name: End of Path
24.description_long: You have come to the end of the path, where it becomes surrounded by groups of trees huddled very close together. Just north of the trees you can see a few of the many huge cliffs and other mountainous regions that make up the western and northern portions of the island.
24.direction_description: You have to go back to the South.

23.description_short: You are standing in an open field.
23.name: Open Field
23.description_long: You are standing in an open field, to the north of the island's expansive forest.
23.direction_description: The densly packed trees of this area prevent you from moving to the south or to the east, so you can only head West along a winding path, or North across the field.

22.description_short: You are behind the huge tree.
22.notes: You have to use the grappling hook to catch the knothole higher up in the tree to move upward.
22.name: Behind Tree (Knothole)

10.transition_text: You climb up the rope tied to your grappling hook.

22.description_long: You are on the north side of the largest tree in the forest. Glancing up at the branches swaying in the cool breeze, you spot a large knothole far above your head.
22.direction_description: Being such a large tree, even the lowest of its branches are out of your reach, so you may not climb Up the tree. You can however, go East to a fork in the road, or North towards a huge boulder.

21.description_short: You are at a fork in the road.
21.name: Two Forks
21.description_long: The road forks here, in two directions.
21.direction_description: You can follow the trail to the West, South, or SouthEast.

20.name: More Beach
20.description_long: You are near the end of the beach.
20.direction_description: The beach extends both South and SouthWest, and ends off to the East.

19.description_short: You are on a large branch.
19.name: Branch - Knothole

11.transition_text: You climb down the rope tied to your grappling hook.

19.description_long: This long and wide branch which extends out from the tree, just below the place where a large knothole has formed. To the south of you a small wooden platform has been in the cleft where two branches grow from the tree's vast trunk.
19.direction_description: You can try to climb Down the tree, or work your way over to the small platform, just South of here.

18.description_short: You are on a small wooden platform.
18.name: Small Wooden Platform
18.description_long: You are now standing on a small, well-weathered, wooden platform built in the cleft where two branches extend from the forest's largest tree. When you peer over the edge of the platform, you immediately notice just how far away the ground lies below you, and your mind reels with the height.
18.direction_description: You can work you way out North or South along either of the branches.

17.description_short: You are on the bend of a path.
17.name: Bend in Path
17.description_long: Here the path running through the forest bends into a Y-shape. An open field stretches out before you, spilling down the light that is blocked by the canopy of the forest's enormous trees.
17.direction_description: The path ends to the North, continues to the West and NorthWest, and there is an open field to the East.

16.description_short: You are at the base of an enormous tree.
16.name: Huge Tree
16.description_long: An enormous tree is growing in the middle of the path here; you can not continue in this direction. Your glance follows the trunk up towards the forest canopy, and something catches your eye in the branches high above your head. It is too far up and there is too little light to be certain what it might be..

15.description_short: You are on a low branch.
15.name: Lower Branch
15.description_long: You are standing on one of the lower branches of the tree, overhanging a shady cove just below you.
15.direction_description: All of the other branches are too far above you to climb to, so you will have to climb Down.

14.description_short: You are behind the large oak tree.
14.name: Behind Large Oak
14.description_long: You are on the northern side of a large oak tree. To the north stands a tree with its roots exposed.
14.direction_description: You may head North to the tree with its roots exposed, or South to the other side of this large oak tree.

13.description_short: You are at an intersection of paths.
13.name: Three Forks
13.description_long: Another path intersects here, allowing you to travel in any of four directions.
13.direction_description: You can either head West towards the largest tree in the forest, North to where the path forks, or follow the path to the East or South.

12.notes: The Gate Key is hidden here, in the darkness. You mush Search for it.
12.name: Dark Woods
12.description_long: It is very dark here, since most of the light is blocked out by the trees. You find it very difficult to even see your own hans, let alone the trees all about you.
12.direction_description: The only direction to go is East, where there is more light.

11.description_short: You are at the bottom of a hand-carved staircase.
11.name: Bottom of Temple
11.description_long: You cast your gaze to the south as you walk along the cliffs, only to discover a smooth staircase, hand-carved into the bare rock of a small mountain.
11.direction_description: Your choices are either walking Up the steps to the top of the mountain, East to one end of a rope bridge, or West along the northern cliffs of the island.

10.name: Path
10.description_long: The trees and foliage on either side of the path are very dennse, alowing very little light through.
10.direction_description: Rather than wander away, you'd prefer to stay on the path; therefore you may go either North or South.

9.name: Wash-Up
9.description_long: There is a piece of indistinguishable wreckage being pushed up and down the beach by the waves here. A strange shadow of deju va overcasts your mind as you watch it wash up and down the coastline.
9.direction_description: The beach continues SouthWest and NorthEast, and there is a large tree to the NorthWest.

8.description_short: You are at the start of a path.
8.name: Start of Path
8.description_long: You are at the start of a long path that winds through the forest which now surrounds you on both sides.
8.direction_description: You can follow the path to the North, move South down the beach, head East where a large tree sways gentle in the breeze, or walk into the shade to the West.

7.description_short: You are on top of a rockslide.
7.name: Rope
7.description_long: Apparently there once was a rock-slide here some time ago, making for loose footing. It is impossible to continue up the cliff from this direction.
7.direction_description: You have the choice of either working your way along the edge of the rockslide Southward, or sliding East and down to the shady spot below.

6.description_short: You walking along a cliff above the ocean.
6.name: Impassible Cliff
6.description_long: The ocean's waves are crashing justbelow you, painting patches of slick aglae around the already loose footing. You'd like to leave this area as soon as possible.
6.direction_description: You may only work your way along the cliff to the North.

5.description_short: You are in a shady spot.
5.notes:
First Transition Text:

You slowly work you way up the side of the hill, along the rock slide. As you near the top of the rock slide you see a rope that is dangling down from the clifftop above you. You grab for the rope as you begin to lose your footing, only to hear a loud snapping sound above, followed by the rope and the dead branch it was tied to falling to the ground just in front of you. You end up sliding back down the hill for a short distance, but finally regain control of your equilibrium and reach the top of the rockslide, where the rope and dried-out tree branch are now laying.
5.name: Stranger
5.description_long: The many limbs of the trees that hang far above you block out the sun and cools the hot sand, comforting your feet.
5.direction_description: The beach ends to the South and continues to the SouthEast. A pathway leading into the forest begins to the East, and an old rock slide lies just West of here.

4.description_short: You are standing in front of a small alcove.
4.name: Outside Alcove

2.transition_text: You walk up the beach until you reach the point where the forest overhangs the sand.

4.description_long: You are standing along the edge of the beach, in front of a small alcove, formed in the space between the cliff and a large rock. To the west looms a sharply inclined cliff.
4.direction_description: You can venture West into the alcove, NorthEast along the coastline, or North towards a shady spot.

3.description_short: You are at the base of a large oak.
3.name: Front of Large Oak
3.description_long: There is a large oak tree here, one of the largest in the forest.
3.direction_description: The wind blows, russling the branches on the North side of the tree. A pathway leading into the forest begins to the West, and the beach runs South, SouthWest, and South East of here.

2.name: Walking Along Beach
2.description_long: You are walking along the beach, the waves crashing to the south.
2.direction_description: You can see the beginning of a forest to the North, and the beach extends both NorthEast and NorthWest.

1.name: Beachfront

2.transition_text: As you step into the shade of the forest, your eyes take a moment to adjust to the dim light.

1.description_long: You are standing on a large beachfront. In front of you looms a great forest, populated with a wide assortment of trees, including a few abnormally tall ones. You notice that the western portion of this island is made up of a series of cliffs and rocky terrain that gently slopes down towards the lush green portions of the island to the east.
1.direction_description: You can see a shady spot under a tree to the NorthWest, a forest starts to the NorthEast, and the beach extends both SouthWest and SouthEast. There is also a path that leads into the forest to the North.

83.aliases: rope, tree branch, wood, rope tied to wood, branch
83.name: Rope Tied to Tree Branch
83.description: A long length of rope is tied around the tree branch

82.description: The red orb, glowing softly with an inner light, is inside this plain wooden bucket.
82.name: Bucket (with Red Orb inside)

81.aliases: object, dark object, object on the forest floor, object on the floor
81.description: There is a dark object somewhere around here, on the forest floor.
81.name: Dark object on the forest floor

80.aliases: blue, pedestool, blue metal, blue dome, dome, metal, blue orb, orb
80.description: This pedestool has a deep blue hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome. The Blue Orb is now engaged by the metal, glowing brightly as it casts beams of light about the room.
80.name: Blue Pedestool (with Blue Orb)

79.aliases: red, pedestool, red metal, red dome, dome, metal, red orb, orb
79.description: This pedestool has a bright red hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome. The Red Orb is now engaged by the metal, glowing brightly as it casts beams of light about the room.
79.name: Red Pedestool (with Red Orb)

78.aliases: black, pedestool, black metal, black dome, dome, metal, black orb, orb
78.description: This pedestool has a dark black hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome. The Black Orb is now engaged by the metal, glowing brightly as it casts beams of light about the room.
78.name: Black Pedestool (with Black Orb)

77.aliases: white, pedestool, white metal, white dome, dome, metal, white orb, orb
77.description: This pedestool has a bright white hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome. The White Orb is now engaged by the metal, glowing brightly as it casts beams of light about the room.
77.name: White Pedestool (with White Orb)

76.aliases: blue, pedestool, blue metal, blue dome, dome, metal
76.description: This pedestool has a deep blue hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome.
76.name: Blue Pedestool

75.aliases: red, pedestool, red metal, red dome, dome, metal
75.description: This pedestool has a bright red hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome.
75.name: Red Pedestool

74.aliases: black, pedestool, black metal, black dome, dome, metal
74.description: This pedestool has a dark black hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome.
74.name: Black Pedestool

73.aliases: white, pedestool, white metal, white dome, dome, metal
73.description: This pedestool has a bright white hue to it. There is a metal band across the top, which is curved inwards, like an inverted dome.
73.name: White Pedestool

72.aliases: meat, albatross, skinned albatross, bird, dead bird, dead albatross, meaty, skinned meat, dagger
72.description: You first skinned the dead albatross, and now have wrapped up your dagger inside of the meat.
72.name: Skinned Meat (with Dagger)

71.aliases: meat, albatross, skinned albatross, bird, dead bird, dead albatross, meaty
71.description: You have skinned the dead albatross, leaving you with the meat from the bird.
71.name: Skinned Meat

70.aliases: albatross, bird, dead bird, dead albatross
70.description: This bright ambassador of morning is no more.
70.name: Albatross (Dead)

69.description: Overhead the albatross hangs motionless upon the air.
69.name: Albatross

68.description: This is a large metal key. It feels very heavy in your hand.
68.name: Large key (Duplicate)

67.description: There is a plate on the top of the pedestool, where you found the White Orb.
67.name: Pressure Plate

66.description: These are two wooden peg, imbedded firmly into the ground. The rope bridge, now dangling on the eastern side of the cliff, used to be attached to them.
66.name: Two Pegs

65.notes: Gets added to the Bottom of the Well after the crank is used.
65.description: One end of this chain dangles freely in the air, while the other is attached to the crank at the top of the well.
65.name: End of chain

64.aliases: In the middle of the gate is situated a piece of wood, carved into the shape of the face of a troll.
