Strangly, the face looks exactly how you had pictured the troll from a storybook your were read when you were very young. The troll's face has its eyes closed, it's mouth open, and it's tognue sticking out.troll face gateway, troll face gate, troll gate, face gate, gate, troll, lock, brass gate, tongue, face, mouth, open mouth
64.description: This gateway used to block your pathway across the bridge. In the middle of the gate is situated a piece of wood, carved into the shape of the face of a troll. Strangly, the face looks exactly how you had pictured the troll from a storybook your were read when you were very young. The troll's face has its eyes open, it's mouth closed, and seems to be grinning at you.
64.name: Trollface Gateway (Opened)

63.aliases: Grappling Hook, Hook, Rope, Tree, Stump, Tree Stump
63.name: Grappling Hook (Attached to Tree Stump)
63.notes: You can Split back into a Grappling Hook and a Tree Stump
63.description: You have attached your grappling hook onto one end of an old gnarled tree stump.

62.name: Cylindrical Pedestool (With Brick)
62.description: This is a long cylindrical pedestool that is topped with four metal spikes that curve inwards to this room's proportions. You have placed a brick of an approximately similar shape and mass on top of the pedestool to act as a counter weight for the trap-door mechanism.

61.name: Cylindrical Pedestool
61.description: This is a long cylindrical pedestool that is topped with four metal spikes that curve inwards to this room's proportions.

60.name: Boulder (Rolled Aside)
60.description: The large boulder, which had once blocked all passage to the north has rolled aside far enough for you to squeeze your way through.

59.aliases: Column, Pillar, Exotic Seashell, Seashell, Shell
59.name: Column (With Exotic Seashell)
59.description: This is a short and stubby cylindrical column with extremely elaborate marks and patterns etched onto its surface. You have placed the exotic seashell on to of the column, which has resulted in the reflection of the sun's rays all about the area on top of the mountain, with a concentration on the large cylindrical pillar in the center of the area.

58.aliases: Pillar, marks, patterns, hole, center, cone-shaped hole, cone, cone-shape
58.name: Column
58.description: This is a short and stubby cylindrical column with extremely elaborate marks and patterns etched onto its surface. There is a round, cone-shaped hole in the center of the column.

57.name: Decorated Stone Pedestool
57.description: This is a small stone pedestool, covered with various forms of bizarre pictures and markings, whose possible meaning utterly eludes you.

56.name: Ornate Spear (Imbeded in Decorated Stone Pedestool)
56.description: This is a long spear with very ornate carvings running down the wooden handle, imbeded in a small stone pedestool.

55.name: Pedestool
55.description: This is one of four multi-colored pedestools, one white, one black, one blue, and one red.

54.name: Red Orb (Inside Wooden Bucket)
54.description: You are carrying the red orb in the wooden bucket that you took from the well. The orb is a perfectly sphereical crystaline orb about the size of your hand, if you spread your fingers out wide. The orb glows slightly with a red light that eminates from it's center.

53.name: The Red Orb
53.description: This is a perfectly sphereical crystaline orb about the size of your hand, if you spread your fingers out wide. The orb glows slightly with a red light that eminates from it's center.

52.name: The Blue Orb
52.description: This is a perfectly sphereical crystaline orb about the size of your hand, if you spread your fingers out wide. The orb glows slightly with a blue light that eminates from it's center.

51.name: The Black Orb
51.description: This is a perfectly sphereical crystaline orb about the size of your hand, if you spread your fingers out wide. The orb glows slightly with a black light that eminates from it's center.

50.name: The White Orb
50.description: This is a perfectly sphereical crystaline orb about the size of your hand, if you spread your fingers out wide. The orb glows slightly with a white light that eminates from it's center.

49.aliases: Cable, Rung, Ring, Metal Ring, Hoop, handle
49.name: Metal Cable (Tied to Metal Rung)
49.description: You have tied the strong metal cable onto the large grey metal rung that is imbeded into the ceiling of the hand-carved cave that leads into the heart of the volcano.

48.aliases: Rung, Ring, Metal Ring, Hoop, handle
48.name: Metal Rung
48.description: This is a large grey metal rung imbeded into the ceiling of the hand-carved cave that leads into the heart of the volcano. You bring your hand close to the metal surface, expecking to feel an enormous amount of heat radition from the rung, but apparantly it is not made of a heat-conducting material, as it actually feel cool to the touch.

47.name: Multi-Coloured Berries
47.description: This is a small bunch of berries that you have found growing in the wild on the southwestern portion of the island. They appear to have grown in any colour you like.

46.name: Flask (Filled with Luminescent Liquid)
46.description: This is a small metal flask that is currently filled with a luminescent liquid.

45.name: Exotic Seashell (Filled with Luminescent Liquid)
45.description: This curved, exotic seashell is made from a material that is both translucent and highly reflective at the same time. The seashell is currently filled with a pungent-smelling, luminescent liquid that glows very brightly inside of the shell. The curved, translucent surface of the shell manginfies and concentrates the light eminating from within the shell, effectively creating a very handy portabel light source.

44.name: Cauldron (Filled with Luminescent Liquid)
44.description: This is a very heavy fat metal pot, suspended above a small firepit. The cauldron currently contains a pungent-smelling luminescent liquid that glows brightly at the bottom of the small pot.

43.name: Cauldron (Filled with Luminescent Fungi and Unknown Liquid)
43.description: This is a very heavy fat metal pot, suspended above a small firepit. The cauldron currently contains a mixture of phosphorescent fungi and pungent-smelling, unknown liquid. The mixture is ready to be heated.

42.name: Cauldron (Filled with Unknown Liquid)
42.description: This is a very heavy fat metal pot, suspended above a small firepit. The cauldron currently contains a small amount of unknown liquid.

41.name: Cauldron (Filled with Luminescent Fungi)
41.description: This is a very heavy fat metal pot, suspended above a small firepit. The cauldron currently contains a small quantity of phosphorescent fungi that seem to glow with their own inner light.

40.aliases: Pouch, Fungi, mushrooms, mushroom, Animal Skin Pouch, Animal Skin
40.name: Pouch (Filled with Luminescent Fungi)
40.description: This is a small pouch, handsewn from the hide of an animal not totally unlike a deer. It is currently filled with a form of phosphoresant fungi that seem to glow with their own inner light. You have to be careful when handling the fungi however, as they seem to instantly break apart into spores whenever you try to touch them with your bare hands.

39.aliases: Fungi, mushroom, mushrooms, shroom, shrooms, fungus
39.name: Luminescent Fungi
39.description: These are the slightly larger relatives of the fungi that grow naturally in the soil of the underground tunnel system. These phosphoresant fungi seem to glow slightly with their own inner light, and they break apart into tiny spores the instant you try to touch them with your bare hands.

38.name: Firepit (with Leaves and Wood)
38.description: You have spread the broken up piece of a dry tree branch over the coals of the small firepit, and covered the wood with dry forest leaves. The firepit is now prepared to be lit.

37.name: Firepit (with Leaves)
37.description: You have spread out dry forest leaves across the small firepit. You will still need to add wood to burn if you want to start a fire.

36.name: Leaves
36.description: This is as small pile of dry forest leaves.

35.name: Firepit (with Wood)
35.description: You have broken up a dry tree branch over the coals of the small firepit. You will still need to add something to get the fire initially started if you want to start a fire.

34.name: Animal-Skin Pouch
34.description: This is a small pouch that has been hand-sewed from the hide of an animal not totally unlike a deer.

33.name: Firepit
33.description: This is just the burn remaining of a firepit someone had once used for their campsite.

32.name: Cauldron
32.description: This is a very heavy fat metal pot, suspended above a small firepit.

31.name: Tree Branch
31.description: This is a long, dried out tree branch.

30.name: Rock (moved aside)
30.description: This is a large rock, no different from the many others laying around on the sand.

29.name: Unlocked Gate (Opened)
29.description: This is a huge metalic gate with a very large lock positioned in the center of the gate. The gate is currently unlocked and wide open.

28.name: Crank
28.description: This is a long metal crank that hangs over the base of a small well. There is a long chain, permanently attached to the crank, wound around the middle, directly over the opening of the well. The end of the chain has broken off.

27.name: Bucket
27.description: This is a plain wooden bucket, slightly warped from many years of use.

26.name: Large Metal Key
26.notes: Hidden in the Dark Woods (Room 12) Use it to Unlock Gate
26.description: This is a rather large, shiny, brass key, that sparkles in the light.

25.name: Brick
25.description: This is a large, greyish-white brick, that has seen many years abuse from the elements.

24.name: Ornate Spear
24.description: This is a long spear with very ornate carvings running down the wooden handle. The tip of the spear is made of an incredibly sharp metal of an unknown origin.

23.name: Golden Coin
23.description: This is a small shiny golden coin just slightly smaller than your palm. There are only the faintest of outlines what may have once been printed or etched on the surface. The markings are far too faded for you to make out clearly.

22.name: Oxoplant
22.notes:
Allows you to breath underwater

Currently, equipping the oxoplant doesn't seem to be working properly - 2001.06.17
22.description: This is an oddly shaped plant, with white petals. You move your face to smell it's aroma, and it suddenly clamps around your nose and mouth. You cringe, prepared for some sort of violent act, but nothing of the sort occurs. Instead you realize with much excitement that it is providing you with a more than ample supply of oxygen! This wonderful specimen of foliage takes in your carbon dioxide and releases oxygen. You pull it back from your face and it easily detaches itself. You decide to call it an "oxoplant."

21.name: Sledgehammer
21.description: This heavy hammer has a long handle with a comfortable feel to it. With plenty of leverage, it can provide you with a great deal of power, though it would be to slow to be of any use in a fight.

20.name: Flask (Filled with Unknown Liquid)
20.description: This is a small metal flask that is currently filled with an unknown, pungent liquid.

18.name: Grappling Hook
18.notes: Use it to climb the tree and to fix the rope bridge.
18.description: You have tied a rope to the end of the hook you found in the chest on top of the cliff.

17.name: Hook
17.notes: Combine with Rope to form Grappling Hook
17.description: This is a long, triple-pronged metal hook with a loop on one end.

16.name: Metal Cable
16.description: This is a very strong cable, made from a metal that very closely resembles steel, although it is far lighter. There is a loop on end of the cable.

15.name: Unlocked Box (Opened)
15.description: This is a small box, made from a very heavy and durable metal. The box is so heavy in fact, that you cannot even muster the strength to pick it up. The box is currently unlocked and open.

14.name: Unlocked Box (Closed)
14.description: This is a small box, made from a very heavy and durable metal. The box is so heavy in fact, that you cannot even muster the strength to pick it up. The box is currently closed, but unlocked.

13.name: Locked Box
13.notes: You must use the small metal key to open the lock.
13.description: This is a small box, made from a very heavy and durable metal. The box is so heavy in fact, that you cannot even muster the strength to pick it up. The box is currently locked shut, with a small keyhole just above where the lid opens.

12.name: Small Key
12.description: This is a small shiny metal key.

11.name: Exotic Seashell
11.description: This is an extremely exotic seashell, you've never seen anything quite like it in your entire life. The shell itself is rather large, about three times the size of your fist. The shape is vaguely conical at the top, and spherical near the bottom. The material of the shell is both translucent and reflective. The sun's rays dance across its surface.

10.aliases: Knothole, Grappling Hook, Hole, hook, tree
10.name: Knothole (with attached Grappling Hook)
10.description: Your Grappling hook his hanging out of the knothole, allowing you to climb Up and Down the tree.

9.name: Knothole
9.description: There is a large knothole in the tree on this side, far above your head.

8.aliases: Arrow, Tree, Stump, Tree Stump, rope
8.name: Arrow (with Rope) shot into Stump
8.notes: You can Split back into a Rope Tied to Arrow and a Tree Stump
8.description: You have shot an arrow into a tree stump, with a length of rope tied to one end,which you can now use to climb Up or Down the cliff.

7.name: Tree Stump
7.notes: Use the the Rope Tied to Arrow on this
7.description: This is a gnarled old tree stump. You can see a few patches of moss growing on one side.

6.name: Rope Tied to Arrow
6.notes: You shoot this at the tree stump overhanding the cliff in order to reach the campsite.
6.description: You have tied a length of rope to the end of an arrow.

5.name: Rope
5.notes: Can be tied to the arrow or to the hook
5.description: This rope apears to have been handwoven from extremely resiliant natural fibers. Though it is not very thick in circumference, you are fairly certain that it could at least hold your own weight.

4.name: Arrow
4.notes: For use with bow, attach rope, offensive
4.description: This arrow is very long, with a special razor-sharp point. The only odd thing about it is the small notch behind the plume.

3.name: Bow
3.notes: Carried by Savage, kill him with knife to retrieve this.
3.description: This long, strong, and obvoiusly powerful bow feels comfortable in your grip.

2.name: Rock
2.description: This is a large rock, no different from the many others laying around on the sand.

1.name: Dagger
1.notes: Found under the rock in room #7
1.description: This dagger is heavier than you might expect; it seems to be made of a strong and smooth metal that is a blueish-grey and does not shine, unlike any you have ever seen before. The grip is wrapped in some sort of animal hide. This dagger feels very good in your hand.

13.name: Shark
13.description: This is the largest shark you've ever seen in your life. So far it doesn't seem to have noticed your presence. You're afraid however that if you tried to swim further Down in the water, you might draw its attention.

12.name: Stone Wall
12.description: This stone wall blocks your passage though the tunnel leaded upwards.

11.name: Stone Door
11.description: This is a large door, made of stone. It is far too heavy for you to budge.

10.aliases: troll face gateway, troll face gate, troll gate, face gate, gate, troll, lock, brass gate, tongue, face, mouth, open mouth
10.name: Troll Gate (Closed)
10.description: Your route across the bridge is blocked by a thick brass gate. In the middle of the gate is situated a piece of wood, carved into the shape of the face of a troll. Strangly, the face looks exactly how you had pictured the troll from a storybook your were read when you were very young. The troll's face has its eyes closed, it's mouth open, and it's tognue sticking out.

9.aliases: rocks, cave in, cave, obstacle, block, obstruction
9.name: Cave-In
9.notes: How does the player remove the cave in? This is, afterall, a key moment in the game... you enter this room and place the orbs on the pedestools to remove the boulder, but how do you remove the cavein? the sledgehammer? (most likely)
9.description: This is a large pile of boulders and debris, which must have resulted from part of the tunnel collapsing. You can't tell how long ago the cave-in occured, and there's no way you'd be able to remove it by hand, the rocks are simply too big to budge.

7.name: Unlocked Gate (Closed)
7.description: This is a huge metalic gate with a very large lock positioned in the center of the gate. The gate is currently closed, but it is unlocked.

6.name: Locked Gate (Closed)
6.description: This is a huge metalic gate with a very large lock positioned in the center of the gate. The gate is currently closed and locked shut.

5.name: Darkness
5.notes: This "obstruction" could have been implemented in the same manner as (for instance) the distance from the ledge over the river that runs through the mountains to the tree trunk on top of the mountain, but I felt that since the situation in which one needs a portable light source comes up so often, and is sovled by having a certain item as opposed to performing a different task, that implementing Darkness as an invisible obstacle my be better suited than setting up a series of Modifying ToWhichRoom Directional Objects. Implenting Darkness in this way was actually the orginal way in which all such problems where address, and I also wanted to show that there are many different ways to accomplish basically the same thing by mastering the Event Grammar of the COG Engine. By using this implementation, an ObstructedCompassGraphic will show that someone is capable of entering the darkness, but they currently cannot. If such a technique was used in the case of the tree stump example, the player would be given a hint that there must be *some* way to get up there.
5.description: You can't see in the dark, you need light!

4.name: Rope Bridge (Broken)
4.notes: You use the grappling hook on this from the western side to fix it.
4.description: This bridge once spanned a gulf between two cliffs. It is currently hanging down on the Eastern side. It must have become detached somehow on the Western side.

3.name: Boulder
3.notes: You have to use the analog (natural) computer, powered by all four Orbs, to remove this.
3.description: This large boulder blocks all passage to the North.

2.name: Under Construction Sign
2.description: You have reached the halfway point of the Cycon Odyssey. You can't go any further right now, but keep an eye on the News page from the CogEngine [http://cogengine.sourceforge.net/news.html] and watch for updates!

1.name: Stranger
1.notes:
Use the Dagger to attack him, and he will leave the Bow and one Arrow behind.
